feat: add `plugboard process validate` CLI command (#212)

# Summary

Adds a `plugboard process validate` CLI command that checks whether a
YAML process config has valid topology (connected inputs, event
matching, cycle detection) without running the process.

# Changes

- Add `validate` subcommand to `plugboard/cli/process/__init__.py` —
builds the process from YAML config, runs
`validate_process(process.dict())`, exits 0 on success or 1 with
itemized errors on failure
- Add two unit tests: valid config passes, mocked validation errors
produce exit code 1

```bash
$ plugboard process validate tests/data/minimal-process.yaml
Validation passed

$ plugboard process validate bad-config.yaml
Validation failed:
  • Component 'x' has unconnected inputs: ['in_1']
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Model validation (valid model topology etc)</issue_title>
> <issue_description>Implement validation checks in `plugboard-schemas`
for `Process` objects. Start with implementing the following checks:
> 1. Check that all component inputs are connected.
> 2. Check all components with input events, and make sure there exists
in the process a component that outputs the required event type.
> 3. Check for circular connections within the process topology. These
circular loops must only be considered valid if there are
`initial_values` set on an appropriate component input within the loop.
> 
> For (3) we can use Johnson's algorithm to find all simple circuits
within the process. Implement this as a utility in `plugboard-schemas`,
i.e. try not to introduce additional dependencies. Info on the algorithm
can be found at
https://github.com/qpwo/python-simple-cycles/blob/master/johnson.py.</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

- Fixes plugboard-dev/plugboard#95

commit.message:
Fix atomic attempt upsert and reliable missing-attempt detection in step operations (#85)

Three correctness issues in the MongoDB step/attempt upsert layer:
`ensureAttemptExists` had a read-then-write race that could produce
duplicate attempts; `UpsertStepEnd` used `ModifiedCount==0` as a "not
found" error which fires spuriously on idempotent updates; and
`upsertStepInTestAttempt` used the same unreliable `ModifiedCount==0`
signal to detect missing attempts (masked by `$set: {"updated_at": now}`
always modifying the matched document).

## `ensureAttemptExists` — atomic pipeline replaces read-then-write

Replaced `$not: {$elemMatch}` + `$push` with a single aggregation
pipeline update that conditionally appends only when `retry_index` is
absent:

```go
update := bson.A{bson.D{{Key: "$set", Value: bson.M{
    "tests": bson.M{"$map": bson.M{
        "input": "$tests", "as": "test",
        "in": bson.M{"$cond": bson.A{
            bson.M{"$eq": bson.A{"$$test.id", testID}},
            bson.M{"$mergeObjects": bson.A{"$$test", bson.M{
                "attempts": bson.M{"$cond": bson.A{
                    bson.M{"$in": bson.A{retryIndex, existingRetryIndexesExpr}},
                    bson.M{"$ifNull": bson.A{"$$test.attempts", bson.A{}}},
                    bson.M{"$concatArrays": bson.A{...existing..., bson.A{attempt}}},
                }},
            }}},
            "$$test",
        }},
    }},
}}}}
```

## `upsertStepInTestAttempt` — tight filter + `MatchedCount` as signal

The filter now includes `$elemMatch` down to the attempt, so
`MatchedCount==0` reliably means "attempt absent" regardless of the
`$set` side-effect:

```go
filter := bson.M{"_id": runID, "tests": bson.M{"$elemMatch": bson.M{
    "id": testID,
    "attempts": bson.M{"$elemMatch": bson.M{"retry_index": retry_index}},
}}}
```

`MatchedCount==0` → call `ensureAttemptExists` + retry; second
`MatchedCount==0` → return error.

## `UpsertStepEnd` — `MatchedCount` over `ModifiedCount`

Filter tightened to `$elemMatch` all the way to `steps.id`.
`MatchedCount==0` = step not found (hard error). `ModifiedCount==0` =
idempotent no-op (warn only).

## Tests

Added `mongodb_step_begin_test.go` with three integration tests
covering:
- Empty `attempts: []` triggers `ensureAttemptExists` and inserts the
step
- Repeated `ensureAttemptExists` calls are idempotent (no duplicate
attempts)
- Realistic retry scenario: attempt[0] exists, step for attempt[1]
creates attempt[1] without affecting attempt[0]

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: ubermensch01 <1215522+ubermensch01@users.noreply.github.com>

containers when it considers the image unchanged, causing deploys
to silently serve stale code.

commit.message:
Fix audio autoplay — unlock AudioContext on user gesture

Browsers block audio/video playback until user interaction. The intro
video and narration audio were silently failing because the user gesture
context was lost by the time async operations completed.

Fix:
- unlockAudio() creates and resumes AudioContext + plays silent WAV
  to warm up the audio pipeline, called immediately on Start Game click
- initMusic() + unlockAudio() moved to top of startNewGame() so they
  run synchronously in the user gesture handler
- closeNarrative() also calls unlockAudio() as belt-and-suspenders
  (Begin Your Journey click is another user gesture)
- loadGameSlot() calls unlockAudio() too
- Video: try unmuted first, fall back to muted autoplay (browsers
  always allow muted), added playsinline attribute

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: comprehensive CSS redesign — remove AI-generated look

Replace multi-layer gradients, glassmorphism, and rgba transparency overload
with a clean design token system using solid colors and subtle shadows.

Changes across 8 rounds:
- Round 1: New design token system (surface layers, borders, shadows, radius)
- Round 2: Simplified header & navigation (removed backdrop-filter, glass effects)
- Round 3: Card system overhaul (removed ::before overlays, colored left borders)
- Round 4: Home desktop (solid backgrounds, removed decorative pseudo-elements)
- Round 5: Home mobile + responsive (solid backgrounds across all breakpoints)
- Round 6: Reading experience (sentence/principle/callout cards, notes, shelf)
- Round 7: Dark mode rewrite (token-based, no catch-all gradient selector)
- Round 8: Final polish (converted remaining rgba to tokens, consistency pass)

Key metrics:
- radial-gradient: eliminated (0 remaining)
- backdrop-filter: reduced from ~20 to 1 (modal overlay only)
- rgba patterns: reduced from ~400+ to ~168 (remaining are semantic colors,
  dark mode tints, and functional color swatches)
- Design tokens adopted: 82 var(--bg-surface*), 69 var(--border*), 46 var(--shadow*)
- Net: -390 lines (371 insertions, 761 deletions)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

consensus: fix same-height fork trap — ancestor+1 tiebreaker and immediate finality lock override

Two related fixes for the minority-fork restart attack observed on LW-Michigan:

1. Fork-point hash tiebreaker (fork_resolver.rs)
   Previously the tiebreaker compared the TIP hashes of both chains, which are
   hashes of blocks produced at different times after the fork diverged. Now we
   compare the hashes at common_ancestor+1 — the first block each side produced
   after the split. This is more meaningful (both blocks were produced from the
   same parent at the same slot) and harder to game (the tip hash can be
   influenced by blocks produced much later).
   Falls back to tip-hash comparison when ancestor+1 hashes are not available.

2. Same-height finality lock override (blockchain.rs)
   The finality lock escape required total_ahead > 0, which was permanently 0
   in a same-height fork (our height == consensus height — no peer can be
   'strictly ahead' of us). This caused handle_fork() to block forever even
   with 9/11 peers on the consensus chain, triggering de-registration and the
   watchdog restart loop (AV new compound vector).
   Fix adds MIN_SAME_HEIGHT_CONSENSUS_PEERS=3 immediate bypass: when the fork
   is same-height and >=3 peers independently confirm the consensus hash, lower
   the finality lock to common_ancestor and proceed with the reorg immediately,
   without waiting for the 5-minute escape hatch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: deduplicate synonymous parent products before optimize/group

Before optimize or group, a new _deduplicate_parent_products() step uses
Gemini to identify synonymous parent products (e.g. Mauste/Mausteet/
Mausteseos) and merge them into one canonical parent. Children of
non-canonical parents are re-assigned and empty duplicates are deleted.
This prevents products from bouncing between similar categories.

Bumps version 1.14.0 → 1.14.1.

commit.message:
fix: guard module-level Firebase init and Notifications handler

Both ran at import time with no error handling. If Firebase config is
missing (Constants.expoConfig.extra empty in Expo Go EAS Update context)
or expo-notifications fails, the entire app crashed silently before
any React component could mount - hence blank screen with no error shown.

- Firebase.js: wrap initializeApp in try/catch, provide stub fallback
- notificationService.js: wrap setNotificationHandler in try/catch
- App.js: wrap loading state in ErrorBoundary too

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix 403 error tracking + sequential full analysis

- Fix broken error propagation: HTTPError(403) was swallowed by fetch
  methods' except-Exception handlers, preventing _timed_fetch from
  tracking failures. Now fetch methods re-raise HTTPError so _timed_fetch
  properly sets tv_403, data_error, and error telemetry.
- Fix _with_retry to re-raise HTTPError(403) instead of retrying it
  (since _handle_403 already exhausted its own retries).
- Add POST /api/trigger-all endpoint for sequential agent execution
  with progress tracking via GET /api/trigger-all/status.
- Update frontend to use trigger-all with polling progress display.
- Update tests to verify tv_403, tv_403_resources, and fetch stats
  error flags are properly set after 403 exhaustion.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(zigbee): Allow ZigbeeEP implementers to handle raw ZCL commands (#12479)

* feat(zigbee): Add raw command handler for ZigbeeEP

Register a raw ZCL command handler via esp_zb_raw_command_handler_register()
that forwards all incoming ZCL commands to endpoint implementations before
the ZBOSS stack processes them.

This allows ZigbeeEP subclasses to observe and optionally consume cluster-
specific commands (e.g., On/Off, Level Control, Off with effect) that were
previously handled only internally by the stack. By default, the handler
returns false so the stack continues normal processing — existing behavior
is fully preserved.

New API:
- zb_raw_command_t struct with cluster_id, command_id, data, etc.
- ZigbeeEP::zbRawCommand() virtual method for subclass override
- ZigbeeEP::onRawCommand() callback registration for user sketches

Closes https://github.com/espressif/arduino-esp32/issues/12025

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(zigbee): Include zboss_api.h in ZigbeeHandlers for raw command handler

The raw command handler uses ZBOSS internal APIs (zb_zcl_parsed_hdr_t,
ZB_BUF_GET_PARAM, zb_buf_begin, etc.) but ZigbeeHandlers.cpp is compiled
as a separate translation unit by Arduino's build system, so it needs
zboss_api.h included directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(zigbee): Replace raw command handler with privilege + custom cluster commands

Replace ZBOSS-internal raw command handler with two ESP-IDF public API
mechanisms for intercepting ZCL commands:

- Privilege commands (ESP_ZB_CORE_CMD_PRIVILEGE_COMMAND_REQ_CB_ID) for
  intercepting standard cluster commands via addPrivilegeCommand()
- Custom cluster commands (ESP_ZB_CORE_CMD_CUSTOM_CLUSTER_REQ_CB_ID)
  for receiving custom cluster commands automatically

This removes the zboss_api.h dependency and custom zb_raw_command_t
struct, replacing them with ESP-IDF public structs
(esp_zb_zcl_privilege_command_message_t and
esp_zb_zcl_custom_cluster_command_message_t) following the same handler
pattern used by all other ZigbeeEP callbacks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(zigbee): Add Zigbee_Privilege_Command example

Demonstrates how to use addPrivilegeCommand() and onPrivilegeCommand()
to intercept standard ZCL cluster commands (On/Off) before the stack
processes them. Works with the existing Zigbee_On_Off_Switch example
or any Zigbee controller as the sender.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(zigbee): Clarify privilege command delegation in example

Only register "Off with effect" (0x40) as a privilege command to
demonstrate that the stack delegates handling to the application.
Standard On/Off/Toggle commands are left to the stack.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(zigbee): Update readme and brief in sketch

* ci(pre-commit): Apply automatic fixes

commit.message:
fix: validate required params, fix auth detection, normalize snake_case keys

Bug 1: executeOperation now throws on missing required query/body parameters
instead of silently sending the request without them (83 tools affected).

Bug 2: operationRequiresAuth falls back to checking securitySchemes presence
when neither operation nor document defines a security array. Fixes 25 tools
(license, monitor) that were sending unauthenticated requests.

Bug 4 (revised): callMetaTool normalizes snake_case input keys to camelCase
so LLM-generated parameters like service_prefix map correctly to servicePrefix.

Adds 9 new tests (134 total): normalizeKeys, auth fallback, required param validation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(nhentai): ship v2.3.6 NHentai overhaul (#52)

* feat(nhentai): ship v2.3.6 NHentai overhaul

Rebuild NHentai source to the recovered v2.3.6 state with improved UX, search, and stability.

Highlights:

- Upgrade source metadata/capabilities and add Cloudflare bypass providing support.

- Add endpoint-aware burst rate limiting and safer retry behavior for API usage.

- Expand search/filter pipeline with persisted length, favorites, date, and tag filters.

- Improve popular tag loading/caching and multiselect filter handling for Paperback.

- Refine discover/search pagination flow, sort handling, and related ordering behavior.

- Add richer settings UI controls for display, read-state options, and filter tuning.

- Add NHentai statistics/screen-time tracking form and interceptor data accounting.

- Restore NHentai asset bundle updates (including icon) and settings state helpers.

* fix(nhentai): address PR #52 review comments

- Fix timezone issue in formatDate(): new Date('YYYY-MM-DD') parses as UTC,
  causing day shift in US timezones. Add parseStoredDate() helper that uses
  parseLocalDateKey() for date-only strings to ensure local time parsing.

- Fix removeSpaces conditional branches: all branches were identical, making
  the setting ineffective. Now varies examples between 'EN|' and 'EN | '.

- Add FAVORITES_THRESHOLD_MAX_STATE_KEY reset to resetNHentaiSettings() so
  max favorites threshold is cleared on settings reset.

- Add legacy DATE_MIN/MAX_DAYS_STATE_KEY migration to getDaysOldFilterSetting()
  so existing users' filter values are preserved after upgrade.

- Fix ensureInstallDate() to check for undefined instead of 0, so deliberate
  stats reset to 0 doesn't re-seed totalRead from legacy caches.

- Add STATS_MARK_READ_ON_DESC_COUNT_KEY to resetAllStatistics() and use 0
  for numeric counters to prevent re-seeding after reset.

- Fix removeSpecificTags() to normalize tag prefixes (female:, male:, etc.)
  before matching, so UI can delete tags regardless of stored prefix format.

- Add Cloudflare cookie expiry validation in saveCloudflareBypassCookies()
  to skip storing expired cookies, matching other sources' behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Kakarot Bot <devnull@example.com>

commit.message:
Remove standalone Event Hubs and ADX — use Fabric Eventhouse + Eventstreams

Fabric F8 capacity provides:
- Eventhouse with KQL database (replaces standalone Azure Data Explorer)
- Eventstreams with custom app source (replaces standalone Event Hubs)

This eliminates ~$850/mo in redundant Azure services.

- Delete event-hubs.bicep and data-explorer.bicep modules
- Update main.bicep, deploy.sh, init-kql-tables.sh
- Delete live ADX cluster and Event Hubs namespace

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- grade_classification: all return paths

- grade_response_quality: all return paths
- grade_email_triage: all score fields
